items.title: Notice: Notice to exporters 2026/13: declaring exports under OGELs and GEAs on the UK’s customs declarations system
items.summary: The Export Control Joint Unit (ECJU) informs upcoming requirement to add licence references  into the UK’s Customs Declarations System.

items.title: Official Statistics: UK trade in numbers
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s latest trade and investment position, summarising statistics produced by ONS, DBT, and UNCTAD.

items.title: Official Statistics: Large businesses’ payment practices and performance: Statistics (2025)
items.summary:
Annual statistics describing how large UK businesses pay their suppliers, using data reported under The Payment Practices and Performance Reporting Regulations 2017. 

Includes statistics on the number of reports submitted by businesses, the average time to pay, and the proportion of invoices paid late (by number and value).

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment core statistics book
items.summary: A monthly snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment position, summarising trade statistics produced by ONS, HMRC, DBT and others.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA glossary
items.summary: A glossary to explain terms used throughout the combined heat and power quality assurance (CHPQA) guidance notes.

items.title: Transparency data: DESNZ: prompt payment data, January to March 2026
items.summary: How long the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) takes to pay invoices.

items.title: Form: Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs: apply for an exemption or compensation
items.summary: How to apply for an exemption or compensation for a proportion of the indirect costs of funding the Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs.

items.title: Government and Wayve sign partnership to accelerate Britain’s self driving future
items.summary: Partnership will put the UK at the forefront of next generation self-driving technology

items.title: Transparency data: COVID-19 loan guarantee schemes repayment data: December 2025

items.title: Policy paper: Summary of Memorandum of Understanding between DBT and Wayve
items.summary: This Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) summary sets out how the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) and Wayve, within their respective remits, will jointly deliver shared objectives for trade, exports, and investment.

items.summary: Estimates of sub-regional fuel poverty in England in 2024.

items.summary: This factsheet summarises how the UK is supporting Ukraine following the Russian invasion.

items.summary: This competition provides funding for developing technologies that enable the removal of greenhouse gases from the atmosphere in the UK.

items.summary: Headline Household Energy Efficiency statistics under the Energy Company Obligation (ECO) and the Green Deal Framework.

items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Great British Insulation Scheme (GBIS).

items.summary: Information about how the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) works with its public bodies.

items.summary: The governance framework between Great British Energy (GBE) and the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ).

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Weekly road fuel prices
items.summary: Road fuel price statistics providing average UK retail 'pump' prices on a weekly basis.

items.title: Guidance: Taking part in the UK Emissions Trading Scheme markets
items.summary: This guidance explains the operation of the UK ETS markets and how to take part in auctions and in trading on the secondary market.

items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.summary: The programme supports the development and demonstration of heat pump technologies and tools, and solutions for optimised deployment of heat pumps.

items.title: Contracts for Difference: Contract amendments to implement Clean Industry Bonus reforms
items.summary: We're seeking views on proposed amendments to the CfD contract that need to be made to implement CIB reforms, and also on hybrid metering, ahead of Allocation Round 8.

items.title: Guidance: Horizon Shortfall Scheme Appeals (HSSA): tariff of reasonable legal costs
items.summary: This guidance sets out the legal costs we will cover for Horizon Shortfall Scheme Appeals (HSSA) applicants.

items.title: Independent report: Working Lives: the scale and nature of labour market non-compliance in the UK – final report
items.summary: The final report of Director of Labour Market Enforcement (DLME) commissioned assessment on the scale and nature of labour non-compliance and other work-based harms in the UK.

items.title: New legislation gives Government power to bring British Steel into public ownership
items.summary: New legislation to be announced in the King's Speech on Wednesday will give government the option to bring British Steel into public ownership.

items.title: Notice: Hydrogen BECCS Innovation Programme: projects awarded funding
items.summary: This programme supported innovation in hydrogen BECCS (bioenergy with carbon capture and storage) technologies with £31 million in funding.

items.summary: How to apply for financial redress if you experienced financial losses or other harm as a result of your use of the Capture software.

items.title: Jet fuel and travel plans: what you need to know
items.summary: UK airlines say that they are not currently seeing a shortage of jet fuel. The government is working with industry and international partners to keep passengers moving.

items.summary: This summary business case supports the case for investment in extending the Boiler Upgrade Scheme (BUS) to 2029/30.

items.summary: This notice sets out the process for excluded sites to apply for an exemption from their temporary site exclusion for the eighth CfD allocation round.

items.title: Invest 2035: the UK’s modern industrial strategy
items.summary:
The industrial strategy is the UK government’s proposed 10-year plan for the economy. It aims to deliver the certainty and stability businesses need to invest in the high-growth sectors and drive long-term economic growth. 

We want to hear your views on our proposals. 

The final industrial strategy will be published in spring 2025, alongside the multi-year spending review.

items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas.
